channel.title: NPR Programs: Morning Edition
channel.description: Morning Edition takes listeners around the country and the world with multi-faceted stories and commentaries every weekday. Hosts Steve Inskeep, Leila Fadel, Michel Martin and A Martínez bring you the latest breaking news and features to prepare you for the day.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: U.S. and Iran agree to 2-week ceasefire
item.description: The U.S. and Iran reached a ceasefire deal on Tuesday, less than two hours before the deadline President Trump imposed for Iran to meet his demands or else face wide-scale destruction.
item.content:encoded: The U.S. and Iran reached a ceasefire deal on Tuesday, less than two hours before the deadline President Trump imposed for Iran to meet his demands or else face wide-scale destruction.
item.dc:creator: Franco Ordoñez

item.title: After Trump backs off threats against Iran, where does US military operation stand?
item.description: To discuss what's likely to be next for U.S. military strategy in the Iran war, NPR's Leila Fadel speaks with retired Gen. Joseph Votel.
item.content:encoded: To discuss what's likely to be next for U.S. military strategy in the Iran war, NPR's Leila Fadel speaks with retired Gen. Joseph Votel.
item.dc:creator: Leila Fadel

item.title: NATO chief to visit the White House after Trump's threats to leave alliance
item.description: NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte meets with President Trump Wednesday amid tensions over the alliance.
item.content:encoded: NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte meets with President Trump Wednesday amid tensions over the alliance.
item.dc:creator: Teri Schultz

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: U.S. and Iran agree to two week ceasefire, how Iranians are responding to the ceasefire, the effects of the war in Iran give investors around the world whiplash.
item.content:encoded: U.S. and Iran agree to two week ceasefire, how Iranians are responding to the ceasefire, the effects of the war in Iran give investors around the world whiplash.
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep

item.title: 'The Boys' returns for fifth and final season
item.description: NPR's A Martinez talks to television producer Eric Kripke and actor Jessie T. Usher about the final season premiere of the satirical superhero series "The Boys."
item.content:encoded: NPR's A Martinez talks to television producer Eric Kripke and actor Jessie T. Usher about the final season premiere of the satirical superhero series "The Boys."
item.dc:creator: A Martínez

item.title: Trump-backed Clay Fuller wins House race in Georgia to replace Greene
item.description: Clay Fuller received President Trump's endorsement earlier in the election season in the race to replace former Rep. Marjorie Taylor Greene. He will serve out the remainder of Greene's term.
item.content:encoded: Clay Fuller received President Trump's endorsement earlier in the election season in the race to replace former Rep. Marjorie Taylor Greene. He will serve out the remainder of Greene's term.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Kallis

item.title: Photos and data from Artemis II mission could help determine the moon's origins
item.description: The crew of the Orion spacecraft continue to beam back images from their lunar flyby. The photos reveal previously unseen details of the far side of the moon.
item.content:encoded: The crew of the Orion spacecraft continue to beam back images from their lunar flyby. The photos reveal previously unseen details of the far side of the moon.
item.dc:creator: Brendan Byrne

item.title: How Iranians are reacting to news of the two-week ceasefire between the US and Iran
item.description: Iranian state media showed images of Iranians celebrating the news of the ceasefire, but for many, the deal also means an end to any hope of real regime change.
item.content:encoded: Iranian state media showed images of Iranians celebrating the news of the ceasefire, but for many, the deal also means an end to any hope of real regime change.
item.dc:creator: D. Parvaz

item.title: With temporary ceasefire in place, what's Iran's next move?
item.description: With President Trump declaring a two-week ceasefire, NPR's Leila Fadel speaks with Suzanne Maloney, vice president and director for foreign policy at Brookings, about Iran's next moves.
item.content:encoded: With President Trump declaring a two-week ceasefire, NPR's Leila Fadel speaks with Suzanne Maloney, vice president and director for foreign policy at Brookings, about Iran's next moves.
item.dc:creator: Leila Fadel

item.title: American journalist kidnapped by an Iran-backed militia released
item.description: American journalist Shelly Kittleson has been released a week after she was kidnapped by an Iranian-backed Iraqi militant group.
item.content:encoded: American journalist Shelly Kittleson has been released a week after she was kidnapped by an Iranian-backed Iraqi militant group.
item.dc:creator: Jane Arraf
